label: Approval card
description: Shows an approval-style card for a completed workspace action.
llmUsageGuidance: Use when the user wants confirmation, approval, or review framing.
presentationContract: Provide headline, summary, status, fields[], and optional next_step for approval presentation.

label: Bar chart
llmUsageGuidance: Use for comparing values across categories.
presentationContract: Provide series[] plus xLabel and yLabel for categorical chart rendering.

label: Checklist
description: Displays a sequence of checklist items.
llmUsageGuidance: Use when the result is best communicated as a checklist with completion states.
presentationContract: Provide items[] where each item has label, checked, and optional description.

label: JSON fallback
description: Fallback artifact for untyped structured results.
llmUsageGuidance: Use only when no richer artifact type is valid.
presentationContract: Provide tool_name and raw result for fallback inspection.

label: Key value
description: Simple key-value output for failures or generic states.
llmUsageGuidance: Use for blocked actions or simple status output.
presentationContract: Provide tool, status, failure_type, failure_behavior, and message.

label: Line chart
description: Displays trend data over a sequence.
llmUsageGuidance: Use for trends over time or ordered sequences.
presentationContract: Provide series[] plus xLabel and yLabel for trend chart rendering.

label: Markdown
llmUsageGuidance: Use when long-form explanation or formatted prose is the clearest output.
presentationContract: Provide a content string containing markdown-safe text.

label: Project detail
description: Displays one project with its workspace fields and task counters.
llmUsageGuidance: Use after showing, creating, or updating a single project. When the ProjectCrudTool action is show, create, or update, you MUST generate a project_detail marker using the project object returned by the tool. Never print project detail as aligned text columns.
presentationContract: Marker data must be an object with project_id, name, description, color, is_active, tasks_count, completed_tasks_count, and completion_percentage. Example: <runtime-artifact>{"type":"artifact","artifactType":"project_detail","title":"Project detail","data":{"project_id":"project-id","name":"Website redesign","description":null,"color":"#3B82F6","is_active":true,"tasks_count":0,"completed_tasks_count":0,"completion_percentage":0}}</runtime-artifact>

label: Project list
description: Displays workspace projects in a table.
llmUsageGuidance: Use when listing, searching, or comparing multiple projects. When the ProjectCrudTool action is list, you MUST generate a project_list marker using the projects returned by the tool. Never print the project list as a markdown table or aligned text columns.
presentationContract: Marker data must contain columns ["name","is_active","tasks_count","completed_tasks_count","completion_percentage"] and rows[] objects with project_id, name, color, is_active, tasks_count, completed_tasks_count, and completion_percentage. Example: <runtime-artifact>{"type":"artifact","artifactType":"project_list","title":"Project list","data":{"columns":["name","is_active","tasks_count","completed_tasks_count","completion_percentage"],"rows":[{"project_id":"project-id","name":"Website redesign","color":"#3B82F6","is_active":true,"tasks_count":0,"completed_tasks_count":0,"completion_percentage":0}]}}</runtime-artifact>

label: Stats card
description: Shows compact metrics for a task or workspace event.
llmUsageGuidance: Use when the user asks for stats, metrics, or quantitative breakdowns.
presentationContract: Provide items[] where each item includes a label and value, with optional caption.

label: Table
llmUsageGuidance: Use when rows and columns are the clearest presentation format.
presentationContract: Provide columns[] and rows[] arrays for tabular rendering.

label: Task summary
llmUsageGuidance: Use for concise confirmation after a task is created.
presentationContract: Provide task_id, project_id, project_name, title, status, assigned_to, and assigned_to_name for a created task summary card.

label: Website design
description: Displays a high-quality bespoke website design generated as a self-contained HTML document.
llmUsageGuidance: Use after DesignWebsiteTool loads the frontend-design skill. You MUST create a fresh website_design marker by applying the loaded skill to request_context. The design must be a self-contained HTML document string with inline CSS and optional inline JavaScript. Do not use a fixed schema for palette, typography, sections, or cards. Make all visual choices directly in the HTML/CSS.
presentationContract: Marker data must be an object with html and optional title. The html value must be a complete self-contained HTML document or fragment with inline CSS. Do not include markdown fences. Use marker shape: <runtime-artifact>{"type":"artifact","artifactType":"website_design","title":"Website design","data":{"title":"...","html":"<!doctype html><html><head><style>...</style></head><body>...</body></html>"}}</runtime-artifact>
